title: NOC 14301 – Correspondence, Publication, and Regulatory Clerks | Canada Immigration with GTR Immigration
description: Correspondence, Publication, and Regulatory Clerks play a vital role in supporting publishing houses, government agencies, and regulatory bodies by handling documentation, editorial preparation, licensing, and official communications across Canada. Classified under NOC 14301, this occupation has seen strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—offering realistic immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Expansion of media and digital publicationsIncreased regulatory documentation requirementsEntry-level TEER occupation with specialization pathwaysEmployer-supported recruitmentGrowth in licensing and compliance operations

duties: Receive customer advertising orders and prepare copy
Calculate advertising costs and issue invoices
Respond to public inquiries, claims, and complaints
Assist in preparing materials for publication
Proofread for grammar, layout, and typographical errors
File articles relevant to organizations or clients
Compile and process licence, permit, contract, and registration applications
Authorize and issue approved documents and reimbursements
employment_requirements: Additional courses or diplomas in writing, journalism, or related fields (sometimes required)
Prior clerical or administrative experience (often required for senior roles)
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 11,000 new positions for Correspondence, Publication, and Regulatory Clerks, while around 10,900 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Correspondence, Publication, and Regulatory Clerks are in demand across publishing, journalism, regulatory agencies, and government departments nationwide. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is projected to continue over the coming years.
Newspapers and media housesPublishing firms and printing companiesMunicipal, provincial, and federal government officesRegulatory authoritiesArchives and research institutions
Annual salaries typically range between $34,500 and $78,900 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
